properties.remark: NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM REPORTS STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. 20 TO 30 TREES DOWN 30 TO 40 TREES DAMAGED IN A SWATH 150 YARDS WIDE AND 1/2 MILE LONG. ONE TREE FELL ON A BARN, ANOTHER ON A HOUSE AND A THIRD ON A TRAILER. SOME DOWNED TREES UP TO 3 FT IN DIAMETER. TREES LAID OUT IN GENERALLY SAME DIRECTION SW TO NE. ESTIMATED WINDS 70 TO 80 MPH. THERE WERE NO DIRECT WITNESSES. HOME OWNER IN BASEMENT AT THE TIME OF DAMAGE.
REPORTED AT RAWS STATION CRKI1

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM REPORTS STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. 20 TO 30 TREES DOWN 30 TO 40 TREES DAMAGED IN A SWATH 150 YARDS WIDE AND 1/2 MILE LONG. ONE TREE FELL ON A BARN, ANOTHER ON A HOUSE AND A THIRD ON A TRAILER. SOME DOWNED TREES UP TO 3 FT IN DIAMETER. TREES LAID OUT IN GENERALLY SAME DIRECTION SW TO NE. ESTIMATED WINDS 70 TO 80 MPH. THERE WERE NO DIRECT WITNESSES. HOME OWNER IN BASEMENT AT THE TIME OF DAMAGE.
COCORAHS WA-CH-13 REPORTED 1.03 INCHES OF RAIN. STREETS BEGAN FLOODING AROUND 125 PM HALTING TRAFFIC AT INTERSECTIONS. LOWER LEVEL OF OUTBUILDING FLOODED.
1 TO 2 INCES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN THE STREETS CURB TO CURB ON ROADS LEADING OUT OF NUMBER 1 CANYON AND NUMBER 2 CANYON. ROADS ARE NOW CLEAR AS OF 3 PM.
REPORTS OF MANHOLE COVERS BEING BLOWN OFF FROM WATER IN THE MILLERDALE AVE TO SPRINGWATER AVE ALONG MILLER ST
REPORTS OF MANHOLE COVERS BEING BLOWN OFF FROM WATER AND MUD DEBRIS IN STREETS
MUDFLOW ON THE US 2/97 JCT. SR 28

HIGH INTENSITY RAINFALL ON THE 2012 PEAVINE BURN SCAR CAUSED A FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW IN TRONSEN CREEK, AND OVER UPPER TRONSEN RD. DAMAGE INCLUDED WASH OUT OF A PORTION OF UPPER TRONSEN RD AND DEPOSTION OF SEVERAL BOULDERS ALONG THE ROAD.

HIGH INTENSITY RAIN ON THE 2012 PEAVINE FIRE BURN SCAR CAUSE AN EXTENSIVE DEBRIS FLOW IN DEVILS GULCH. A LONG SEGMENT OF A USFS TRAIL/PATH WAS WIPED OUT. SEVERAL HUNDRED LINEAL FEET OF THE TRAIL WAS COVERED SEVERAL FEET DEEP IN DOWNED TREES CARRIED BY THE FLOOD.
MOSTLY PEA-SIZED HAIL OR 1/4 INCH HAIL. A FEW 1.25 INCH HAILSTONES.
ALONG HIGHWAY 2 AND ST ANDREWS ROAD.
LARGE TREE BLOWN DOWN ONTO HIGHWAY 95 AT MILEPOST 533

HEAVY RAIN BACKED UP CULVERTS IN TOWN AND CAUSED MUD SLIDES ONTO ROADS. ONE MUDSLIDE WAS 2-3 FEET DEEP ON LAKE STREET. ANOTHER SLIDE WAS 2 FEET DEEP ON SINLAHEKIN ROAD. THREE HOUSES ON SILVER STREET HAD SOME MINOR WATER DAMAGE.
1 TREE DOWN ACROSS JACKKNIFE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND DIAMETER OF THE TREE UNKNOWN.
WITNESS DESCRIBED A WALL OF WATER, MUD, ROCKS, BRANCHES AND TREE LIMBS COMING DOWN NUMBER 1 CANYON ROAD. HE SAID HE COULD HEAR THE ROCKS SCRAPING ON THE ROAD.
DEBRIS FLOW REACHED INTERSECTION OF LARS LANE AND NUMBER 1 CANYON ROAD.
MUDSLIDE REPORTED FROM CHELAN COUNTY SO. NUMBER 2 CANYON ROAD CLOSED DUE TO MUD AND DEBRIS OVER THE ROAD.
REPORTS OF BASEMENTS BEING FLOODED ALONG WITH OUTBUILDINGS FROM PROLONGED PERIODS OF HEAVY RAINFALL. MUDSLIDES ALSO REPORTED AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAINS.

WINDS UPROOTED A 40 FOOT TREE WITH A 5 FOOT DIAMETER TRUNK ONTO A CAR IN SOUTHEASTERN QUINCY. POWER AND PHONES ARE OUT IN THE SOUTHWESTERN PORTION OF QUINCY. STANDING WATER 6 INCHES IN DEPTH REPORTED ACROSS HIGHWAY 28 IN TOWN. STANDING WATER WAS ALSO REPORTED ACROSS MUCH OF THE OTHER AREAS OF TOWN AS WELL.

RESIDENT REPORTED MINOR FLASH FLOOD JUST UPSTREAM OF KINGSBURY ROAD WHERE COLOCKUM CREEK FLOWS UNDER COLOCKUM RD. CREEK SWELLED OUT OF ITS BANKS AND ACROSS ROADWAY. NO DAMAGE TO ROAD. DAMAGE WAS SUSTAINED TO THE FENCE ADJACENT TO THE CREEK ON THE EDGE OF THE PROPERTY. TIME OF THE EVENT IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
CHELAN COUNTY PUD REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES ON POWER LINES AND POWER POLES BROKEN ON THE SOUTHERN END OF LAKE CHELAN.
2 TREES DOWN AT DESERT CANYONS LODGE. TREES WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 2 FT IN DIAMTER AT THE BASE.

3 LARGE TREES KNOCKED OVER BY WINDS INTO HIGH VOLTAGE POWER LINES NORTH OF ORONDO. DIAMETER OF TREES ESTIMATED TO BE 2 FEET AND AROUND 120FT TALL. SMALL FIRE STARTED DUE TO POWER LINES DOWN. APPLE CROP ALSO DAMAGED DUE TO THE HIGH WINDS.
APPROX 50 FRUIT TREES IN ORONDO WERE UPROOTED OR BROKE AS THE RESULT OF WINDS. TREES 1FT IN DIAMTER SNAPPED.
TREES BLOWN DOWN. TWO OF THE DOWNED TREES LANDED ON HOUSES ALONG E POPLAR ST. MANY LARGE BRANCHES ON GROUND THROUGHOUT WATERVILLE. EVIDENCE OF HAIL BUT NOT CONFIRMED.
CHELAN COUNTY PUD REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES ON POWER LINES AND POWER POLES BROKEN ON THE SOUTHERN END OF LAKE CHELAN.
HIGHWAY 2 AT PINE CANYON WAS CLOSED DUE TO MUD AND DEBRIS COVERING THE ROADWAY FOR A PERIOD OF TIME. ROAD WAS COVERED IN 6 TO 8 INCHES OF DEBRIS.
MUD AND ROCKS WASHED ONTO HIGHWAY 2 CLOSING THE HIGHWAY FOR ABOUT 2 HOURS.
WASHINGTON DOT REPORTED HIGHWAY 97 ALTERNATE AS BEING BLOCKED AT MILEPOST 212 DUE TO A MUDSLIDE.
NORTH CASCADES SMOKEJUMPER BASE RAWS. GUST WAS REPORTED ON THE 106 AM OBSERVATION SO TIME OF THE ACTUAL GUST WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES DAMAGED OR BLOWN DOWN AT BEAVER CREEK GOLF COURSE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WASHINGTON DOT REPORTED HIGHWAY 971 BETWEEN MILEPOST 9-11 HAS MUDSLIDE BLOCKING THE ROADWAY.

ROBINSON CANYON HEAVY DEBRIS FLOW THROUGH RESIDENCE AND ACROSS COLOCKUM RD. HOME HAD WATER FLOWING THROUGH IT AND OUT THE FRONT WINDOWS. RESIDENTS ESCAPED THROUGH FRONT WINDOWS AND CLIMBED TREE UNTIL WATER RECEDED. NEARBY BARN SUSTAINED DMAJOR AMAGE FROM DEBRIS FLOW. ASPHAULT REMOVED FROM COLOCKUM ROAD BETWEEN COLOCKUM CREEK TO KINGSBURY ROAD. KINGSBURY BRIDGE OVER COLOCKUM CREEK SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE.

FLASH FLOODING REPORTED DOWN THE COLOKUM DRAINAGE. WATER RUSHING OVER THE COLOKUM ROAD AT MILE POST 12. THIS SECTION OF THE ROAD HAS BEEN WASHED OUT AND IS IMPASSABLE. A MOBILE HOME ON TARPISCAN ROAD AT THE BOTTOM OF THE DRAINAGE HAS BEEN WASHED OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION. THE RESIDENTS IN THE MOBILE HOME WERE ABLE TO MAKE IT TO HIGHER GROUND BEFORE IT WAS WASHED AWAY.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED DOWN THE DRY GULCH DRAINAGE. FLOOD WATERS AND DEBRIS LED TO THE WASHOUT OF THE DRY GULCH BRIDGE ON COLOCKUM ROAD AND NOW THE ROAD IS IMPASSABLE.

FLASH FLOODING REPORTED DOWN THE COLOKUM DRAINAGE. WATER RUSHING OVER THE COLOKUM ROAD AT MILE POST 12. THIS SECTION OF THE ROAD HAS BEEN WASHED OUT AND IS IMPASSABLE. A MOBILE HOME ON TARPISCAN ROAD AT THE BOTTOM OF THE DRAINAGE HAS BEEN WASHED OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION. THE RESIDENCES IN THE MOBILE HOME WERE ABLE TO MAKE IT TO HIGHER GROUND BEFORE IT WAS WASHED AWAY.

NEWSPAPER REPORTED DAMAGE TO 5 POLES HOLDING TRANSMISSION LINES THAT LED TO LOCAL POWER OUTAGES.
SPOTTER IN FERNWOOD ID REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND GUSTY WIND AND HEAVY RAIN.
TWO LARGE TREES SPLIT...ONE ON GOLF COURSE AND ONE ON 5TH AND SHERLOCK. NO DAMAGE NOTED TO PROPERTY. WIDESPREAD 2-4 INCH BRANCHES ON GROUND AROUND TOWN.

SEVERAL TREES DOWN AROUND TOWN. NO DAMAGE NOTED TO PROPERTY ALTHOUGH UTILITY COMPANY REPORTED TREE ON POWERLINE WITH 29 CUSTOMERS AFFECTED.

SHERRIFFS DISPATCH REPORTS POWER TRANSFORMER AND LINES DOWN ACROSS SR155. ROAD CLOSED FOR 3.5 HOURS.

SHERRIFFS DISPATCH REPORTS NUMEROUS TREES DOWN WITHIN COULEE DAM CITY LIMITS. REPORT INCLUDES TREES BLOCKING ROADS, DOWN ON VEHICLES, BOAT TRAILERS, AND HOMES...DEBRIS ON ROADWAYS, STREET SIGNS DOWN. NO INJURIES REPORTED.
ROOF PARTIALLY TORN OFF BY WIND AT COULEE DAM CASINO. DAMAGE SUSTAINED TO CASINO SIGN. HALF OF ROOF REPORTED DAMAGED AND WATER DAMAGE FROM FOLLOWING RAIN.

REPORTS OF MANY TREES DOWN IN THE 9 MILE FALLS AREA...ESPECIALLY ALONG W CHARLES RD. COMBINATION OF TREES BEING UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. POWER OUTAGES DUE TO TREES ON POWER LINES AS WELL AS TREES FALLING ON ROOFS, OUTBUILDINGS, AND FENCES.

TIME ESTIMATED. WENATCHEE WORLD NEWSPAPER REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN OMAK AND OKANOGAN. ROOF DAMAGE TO OKANOGAN HIGH SCHOOL.
COTTONWOOD TREES UPROOTED AND SHINGLES BLOWN OFF OF ROOF ON OMAK RIVER RD. TIME ESTIMATED.
WIND SPEED MEASURED WITH A DAVIS VANTAGE VUE. TIME ESTIMATED.
HAIL WAS MOSTLY HALF INCH IN DIAMETER BUT THERE WERE SOME HAIL STONES UP TO 1.5 INCHES. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ESTIMATED ABOUT 1 INCH OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES WHICH CAUSED SOME MUD TO WASH DOWN OVER A PAVED ROAD. WIND GUSTS TORE SOME SMALL BRANCHES OFF OF TREES.
6 INCH LIMB BROKE OFF A MAPLE TREE AND LANDED ON THE FENCE DAMAGING IT. ALSO THE POWER LINE TO THE HOUSE WAS PULLED OFF BY THE WIND. HEAVY RAIN BUT NO HAIL.
STRONG GUST FRONT ARRIVED BEFORE THE RAIN STARTED. WIND DAMAGED SOME TREES WITH LIMBS UP TO 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER BROKEN.
AT LEAST 10 TREES WERE BLOWN OVER IN TOWN WITH MORE DAMAGE TO OTHER TREES.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS ROAD WASHED OUT IN THE COLOCKUM CREEK AREA. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS TARPISCAN ROAD WASHED OUT AT COLOCKUM CREEK BRIDGE. COLOCKUM AND KINGSBURY ROADS ALSO CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOOD DAMAGE. DAMAGE TO HOMES AND PROPERTY IN THE COLOCKUM CREEK DRAINAGE. AGNEW RD ALSO CLOSED. TIME IS ESTIMATED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS FLASH FLOODING OUT OF NUMBER ONE CANYON AND DOWN 5TH ST. SEVERAL ROAD CLOSURES. REPORTS OF WATER IN AT LEAST 2 HOMES. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW FROM 4TH OF JULY CREEK ABOVE ICICLE CREEK HAS CLOSED NATIONAL FOREST ROAD 76. MANY CAMPERS ARE ABOVE THE ROAD CLOSURE AND UNABLE TO LEAVE UNTIL ROAD IS CLEARED. TIME ESTIMATED.
MUD AND DEBRIS BLOCKING EAGLE CREEK ROAD. ONE LANE HAS BEEN CLEARED TO ALLOW TRAFFIC TO PASS. TIME ESTIMATED.
DEBRIS FLOW HAS CLOSED NATIONAL FOREST ROAD 76.MANY CAMPERS ARE ABOVE THE ROAD CLOSURE AND UNABLE TO LEAVE UNTIL ROAD IS CLEARED. TIME ESTIMATED
MUD AND DEBRIS BLOCKING EAGLE CREEK ROAD. ONE LANE HAS BEEN CLEARED TO ALLOW TRAFFIC TO PASS. TIME ESTIMATED.
DOWNED TREE BLOCKED ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

*** 2 INJ *** TREE FELL ON MOBILE HOME BRIEFLY TRAPPING OCCUPANTS. NEWSPAPER REPORTED THAT THE BEDROOM DOOR WAS DAMAGED SO OCCUPANTS HAD TO CRAW THROUGH OPENING TO HALLWAY AND THEN WAS ABLE TO ESCAPE. MINOR INJURIES SUSTAINED TO ADULT FEMALE AND CHILD.
SEVERAL FLASH FLOODS AND DEBRIS FLOWS OVER 3 MILES OF MISSION CREEK ROAD FROM MILEPOST 4 TO MILEPOST 7. TIME ESTIMATED

FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW FROM IMUS CREEK NEAR THE STEHEKIN LANDING COVERED APPROXIMATELY 350 LINEAR FEET OF STEHEKIN VALLEY ROAD IN MUD AND DEBRIS, AS MUCH AS 8 FEET DEEP IN SPOTS. SEVERAL VEHICLES AND BICYCLES IN THE PARKING AREA NEARBY WERE DAMAGED AND/OR PUSHED INTO THE LAKE.AN OFFICE AND A RESIDENCE BUILDING WERE SURROUNDED BY MUD AND DEBRIS BUT ONLY RECEIVED MINIMAL INTERIOR FLOOD DAMAGE.

DURING STORM SURVEY, NOTED ROAD HAD BEEN COVERED BY WATER AND MUD FROM PREVIOUS NIGHTS STORM. MUD COVERED ROAD FOR APPROXIMATELY 100-150 YARDS AND APPEARED TO BE DEEP IN SECTIONS. NO REPORTS FROM MEDIA OR LAW ENFORCEMENT BUT ROAD WAS LIKELY IMPASSABLE FOR SOME TIME. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
WSP REPORTS THAT HIGHWAY 20 IS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOWS BLOCKING THE ROAD FROM MILEPOST 147 TO 171.
WSP REPORTS THAT HIGHWAY 20 IS CLOSED DUE TO DEBRIS FLOWS AND MUD BLOCKING THE ROAD FROM MILEPOST 147 TO 171.
FLASH FLOOD WASHED OUT APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS OF PORTION EASTBOUND LANE OF HIGHWAY 20 AT MILEPOST 266. ONE LANE WAS KEPT OPEN FOR TRAFFIC.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. TREES DOWN ON HOMES AND FENCES. ESTIMATED 40 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF.
